title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Clark questions Emma regarding her activities on December 20th. Emma explains that she attended a church restoration fund meeting in Blakelockton, which ended at 1:15 p.m., followed by lunch with Mrs. Oudington and Miss Bartlett at the Cadina restaurant. She spent the afternoon shopping in Greenford, Leesworth, and Bots, had tea at the Three Leaves Tea House at 5:15 p.m., and picked up Brian before returning home at 6:00 p.m. She notes her father was angry because Miss Hart failed to arrive to make tea. Emma also mentions her brothers: Alfred arrived on Saturday evening after calling home, and Harold arrived on the night before Christmas.

Mr. Craddock presents Emma with an empty envelope found outside the manor, which Emma recognizes as her own handwriting from a letter written to Matilda. This discovery leads to the possibility that the person in the coffin is indeed Matilda. Elsewhere, Crocker receives a telegram from Amund Dessen confirming a yacht story involving Anna Stravinsky, who has arrived in Jamaica.

In a separate setting, Alexander and Lucy pack for a departure involving a Rolls-Royce. Alexander discusses his father, Brian, a brave fighter pilot, expressing a desire for him to find a stable family life and a good wife. He suggests that Brian likes Lucy and implies he would be open to her being a stepmother.

Finally, Wetherell and Clutter review an investigation report regarding Harold’s alibi for December 20th. While Harold was seen at Sotheby's auction house at 3:30 p.m., he left shortly after. His butler claims he returned home around 7:15 p.m. to prepare for a 7:30 p.m. gala, though the butler cannot confirm the exact time of arrival. Harold also attended a dinner at Cartel Hall but left before the speech concluded.
